skill.name: ai-maestro-agent-messaging
skill.description: Coordinate AI Maestro inter-agent messages with inbox checks, message reading, file-based delivery, and tmux notifications.
skill.summary: Send, receive, and triage AI Maestro messages between coding agents.
skill.author: 23blocks-OS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto patterns. Most command hits are markdown examples for the intended AI Maestro messaging workflow, and the weak-crypto alerts appear to be false positives. Real risk remains from Bash execution, hidden home-directory message access, and optional remote host messaging, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Based Messaging Commands Require Trusted Environment
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash and directs agents to run AI Maestro helper scripts and tmux commands. This is intended functionality, but it allows command execution from marketplace instructions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples confirm intentional Bash execution. The commands are named AI Maestro helpers, so this is legitimate but security-sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Message Access
medium_findings.description: The skill reads AI Maestro inbox and host data from hidden directories under the user home directory. It repeatedly scopes access to the current agent, but private messages can still be exposed if identity detection is wrong.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple documented commands access ~/.aimaestro and derive the agent from tmux. The skill also warns not to read other inboxes, reducing but not removing risk.

medium_findings.title: Local and Remote Messaging Endpoints
medium_findings.description: The skill documents PATCH and curl checks against localhost and configured remote AI Maestro hosts. Message contents may leave the local machine when cross-host messaging is enabled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network behavior is explicit and tied to AI Maestro messaging. I found no malicious endpoint, but remote host delivery is a real data disclosure boundary.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography hits occur on prose and messaging examples. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic API use in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review of the cited lines shows normal documentation text and examples, not cryptographic code. No evidence found for weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Examples Are Operational References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to localhost, an example remote host, and project documentation. They are not evidence of covert exfiltration by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible configuration and reference examples. Remote host use still matters, so the network concern is captured separately as medium risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Tooling
dangerous_patterns.description: The front matter allows Bash, and the body instructs agents to run shell commands for messaging tasks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Bash permission and command usage are directly visible. The pattern is expected for this skill, but it raises execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Substitution for Agent Identity
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses tmux command substitution to build inbox paths for the current agent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The substitution is documented and purposeful. Risk depends on tmux session naming and path handling by the surrounding scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Host Message Delivery
dangerous_patterns.description: Cross-host messaging resolves configured host URLs and sends messages to remote AI Maestro API endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes remote API delivery. No malicious destination is shown, but message data can cross machine boundaries.

content.value_statement: Multi-agent coding work needs clear handoffs and alerts. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code check inboxes, read messages, and notify other AI Maestro agents.
actual_capabilities: Check unread messages for the current AI Maestro agent.
Read specific messages and mark them as read.
Send structured file-based messages to local or configured remote agents.
Send tmux notifications for urgent handoffs.
Guide message priority, type, and agent addressing choices.
limitations: Requires AI Maestro helper scripts, tmux, and message directories.
Uses Bash commands and local files, so trusted workspaces are required.
Remote messaging depends on configured hosts and reachable APIs.
Does not add authentication or encryption beyond the AI Maestro environment.

use_cases.title: Route Backend Requests
use_cases.description: Ask a backend agent for an endpoint, then receive implementation details through the agent inbox.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers using multi-agent coding workflows

use_cases.title: Coordinate Project Status
use_cases.description: Send progress updates, blockers, and completion notices between specialized coding agents.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads managing AI agent teams

use_cases.title: Alert Distributed Agents
use_cases.description: Notify agents on local or configured remote hosts when urgent work needs attention.
use_cases.target_user: Developers running AI Maestro across machines

prompt_templates.title: Check Inbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my AI Maestro agent inbox and summarize any unread messages.

prompt_templates.title: Send Basic Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a normal priority message to backend-api asking whether the user endpoint is ready.

prompt_templates.title: Respond to Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the latest message from frontend-dev, then send a response with the implementation status and file locations.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Cross-Host Alert
prompt_templates.prompt: Send an urgent cross-host message to crm-api@mac-mini, then use a tmux alert if the local session exists.

output_examples.output: I found two unread messages for this agent. One is high priority from backend-api, and one is a normal status update.

output_examples.input: Tell frontend-dev that the login endpoint is ready.
output_examples.output: Message sent to frontend-dev with normal priority. The subject says the login endpoint is ready.

output_examples.input: Alert backend-api about the production issue.
output_examples.output: Urgent notification sent to backend-api. A detailed follow-up message was also placed in the agent inbox.

best_practices: Check the current agent inbox before starting coordinated work.
Use file-based messages for details and tmux alerts for urgent attention.
Confirm the target agent and host before sending remote messages.
anti_patterns: Do not read another agent inbox unless the operator explicitly authorizes it.
Do not send secrets through messages without confirming the environment policy.
Do not use urgent priority for routine status updates.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI agent check its AI Maestro inbox and send messages to other AI Maestro agents.

faq.question: Does it communicate with the human operator?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for agent-to-agent communication inside an AI Maestro workflow.

faq.question: Why does it need Bash?
faq.answer: The documented workflow uses shell helper scripts, tmux commands, local files, and curl checks.

faq.question: Can it send messages to remote machines?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use configured AI Maestro hosts when cross-host messaging is enabled.

faq.question: Is it safe for any workspace?
faq.answer: Use it only in trusted AI Maestro environments because it reads local message files and runs shell commands.

faq.question: Can it mark messages as read?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes reading messages and marking them as read through helper scripts or the local API.
